source.page_label: Hunt | Hunting | South Dakota Game, Fish, and Parks
source.update_note: No explicit last-updated date found. Page footer reads © Copyright 2026 State of South Dakota. Regulations sourced from 2025 South Dakota Hunting and Trapping Handbook (valid through Jan. 31, 2026 for 2025 licenses; Jan. 31, 2027 for 2026 licenses).

species.name: Ring-necked Pheasant
species.hunting_units: Statewide (some game bird refuges open Dec. 1 or Dec. 8)

sub_seasons.name: Youth Season
sub_seasons.note: Ages 12–17; youth under 16 must be accompanied by an unarmed adult; 10 a.m. CT opener.

sub_seasons.name: Resident-Only Season
sub_seasons.note: South Dakota residents only; public lands only; 10 a.m. CT opener.

licenses.name: Habitat Stamp
licenses.covers: Required for all hunters age 18+ before purchasing any hunting license (one per license year). Not required for 1-day, youth, or mentored licenses.

licenses.name: Small Game License*
licenses.covers: Pheasant, grouse, partridge, quail, cottontail rabbit, tree squirrel, and predator/varmint species. Full season for residents.

licenses.name: Resident Combination (Small Game + Fishing)*
licenses.covers: Residents 18+: small game hunting and fishing privileges combined.

licenses.name: Resident Senior Combination (65+)*
licenses.covers: Same privileges as Combination license; for residents age 65 or older.

licenses.name: Resident 1-Day Small Game
licenses.covers: Residents 18+; one daily limit per species; no Habitat Stamp required.

licenses.name: Youth Small Game (ages 12–17)
licenses.covers: Resident and nonresident youth ages 12–17; no Habitat Stamp required.

licenses.name: Mentored Small Game (under age 16)
licenses.covers: Youth under 16 hunting with an unarmed adult mentor; no Habitat Stamp required.

licenses.name: NR Shooting Preserve — 1-Day*
licenses.covers: Nonresident small game hunting on licensed shooting preserves; one day.

licenses.name: NR Shooting Preserve — 5-Day*
licenses.covers: Nonresident small game on licensed shooting preserves; five days.

licenses.name: NR Shooting Preserve — Season*
licenses.covers: Nonresident small game on licensed shooting preserves; full season.

sources.label: SD GFP Pheasant Page
sources.evidence: Only male (rooster) pheasants may be harvested; hens are fully protected across all seasons.

key_notes.title: Pheasant Shooting Hours Start at 10 a.m. CT

sources.label: 2025 SD Hunting and Trapping Handbook (pp. 26–29)
sources.evidence: "Shooting hours: 10 a.m., Central Time, to sunset." Unique restriction — does not follow sunrise rule.

key_notes.title: Grouse, Partridge & Quail: Sunrise to Sunset

sources.label: 2025 SD Hunting and Trapping Handbook
sources.evidence: Shooting hours: Sunrise to sunset statewide for prairie grouse, ruffed grouse, partridge/chukar, and quail.

key_notes.title: Sage Grouse Season Closed — Population Below Threshold

sources.label: SD GFP Sage Grouse Page
sources.evidence: Season closed. Minimum threshold is 300 males on all leks for two consecutive years before a resident-only season will be considered. 2024 count: 94 males on 17 leks.

key_notes.title: Non-Toxic Shot Required on Most Public Lands

sources.label: 2025 SD Hunting and Trapping Handbook
sources.evidence: Non-toxic shot is required to hunt small game on most public lands (WPAs, National Grasslands, and other designated public lands).

key_notes.title: Nonresident Small Game Limited to Two 5-Day Periods

sources.label: SD GFP License Types & Costs
sources.evidence: Nonresident Small Game license is described as '10-days, two 5-day periods' — not a full-season license.

key_notes.title: Youth Under 16 Must Be Accompanied by Unarmed Adult

sources.label: SD GFP Pheasant Page
sources.evidence: All youth hunters under age 16 must be accompanied by an unarmed adult during the youth pheasant season.

key_notes.title: Resident-Only Pheasant Season — Public Lands Only (Oct. 11–13)

sources.label: 2025 SD Hunting and Trapping Handbook (pp. 27–28)
sources.evidence: The 3-day early resident-only season is restricted to public lands statewide and is not open to nonresidents. Possession limit is reduced to 9 birds.

key_notes.title: Game Bird Refuges Have Later Opening Dates

sources.label: 2025 SD Hunting and Trapping Handbook
sources.evidence: Renziehausen GPA, Gerken GBR, and White Lake GBR open Dec. 1 – Jan. 31. Sand Lake NWR (Brown County) opens Dec. 8 – Jan. 31.
